document.title: Property Type Crosswalk — Heritage Gold → LAWIM V2
document.description: Complete semantic harmonization crosswalk between Heritage Gold property model and current LAWIM V2 implementation

mapping_statuses.NORMALIZED_MATCH: Concepts match after normalization (case, format, encoding)
mapping_statuses.ONE_TO_MANY: One Gold concept maps to multiple V2 concepts
mapping_statuses.MANY_TO_ONE: Multiple Gold concepts map to one V2 concept
mapping_statuses.EXTENSION_REQUIRED: Concept does not exist in V2 — requires new implementation
mapping_statuses.OBSOLETE: Gold concept is no longer relevant

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 7 explicit families with sub-referentials; V2 has no family concept. Family can be inferred from property_type but is not validated.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — family must be derived from property_type during migration; 16+ type values must map to 7 families
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — no family validation at creation time; family-based business rules cannot execute
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — 107 qualification matrices are family-specific; cannot qualify without family
entries.search_impact: MEDIUM — searches by family are impossible; must search by specific type strings
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — matching engine assigns property_type weight 15% but cannot use family-level scoring
entries.CRM_impact: MEDIUM — CRM scoring includes property_type_match (20%) but no family dimension
entries.recommended_resolution: Add property_family enum to schema.prisma with values: residential, commercial, industrial, land, agricultural, hotel, project. Validate in property_domain.py.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold commercial family has 16 matrix types; V2 has no family boundary — boutique/office/warehouse are all free-form strings
entries.recommended_resolution: Include in property_family enum as 'commercial'

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has dedicated industrial family with specific fields (access_camion, hauteur_sous_plafond, charge_sol, quai_chargement, puissance_electrique)
entries.data_migration_impact: LOW — few or no existing industrial properties
entries.runtime_impact: MEDIUM — no industrial-specific validation or fields
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — cannot qualify industrial properties without dedicated matrix
entries.matching_impact: MEDIUM — entrepot maps to warehouse but lacks industrial context
entries.recommended_resolution: Include in property_family enum as 'industrial'. Add industrial-specific fields to metadata_json schema.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 7 land subtypes (titre, non_titre, loti, non_loti, titre_collectif, titre_individuel, sous_morcellement). V2 treats 'land' as just another type string. Gold has land-specific fields: surface, is_agricultural, is_constructible, has_water, has_electricity.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — existing 'land' entries must be subtyped
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — no land-specific validation (title status, serviced vs unserviced)
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — 7 land qualification matrices cannot be applied
entries.search_impact: HIGH — cannot filter by title status or land type
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — matching model includes title_status (5%) but no land subtype matching
entries.CRM_impact: MEDIUM — land_buyer lead type exists but cannot be matched to land subtypes
entries.recommended_resolution: Include in property_family enum as 'land'. Store land subtype in metadata_json. Add title_status field.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has dedicated agricultural family with sub-referentiel; V2 has no concept of agricultural property
entries.recommended_resolution: Include in property_family enum as 'agricultural'. Create sub-referentiel mapping.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold treats hotel as its own family; V2 has no hotel-specific type. Hotel appears as COM-MATRIX-010 in commercial matrices.
entries.recommended_resolution: Include in property_family enum as 'hotel'. Map commercial.hotel to this family.

entries.current_concept: not represented as property; Project model exists separately
entries.semantic_differences: Gold treats development projects as a property family; V2 has a separate Project model for investment/development tracking
entries.qualification_impact: MEDIUM — investment matrices (COM-MATRIX-017 to 021) tied to project properties
entries.recommended_resolution: Keep as separate model. Link Property to Project for development properties.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 5 apartment subtypes (non_meuble, meuble, courte_duree, residence_meublee). V2 stores all as free string with no subtype distinction. Gold requires bedrooms/bathrooms for apartments; V2 has generic bedrooms/bathrooms fields.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — existing 'apartment' entries need subtype extraction from metadata or summary
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — 6 apartment-related matrices cannot be applied by subtype
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — matching at 'apartment' level only; subtype matching impossible
entries.CRM_impact: MEDIUM — cannot recommend based on furniture preference
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'apartment' in property_type. Store subtype (non_meuble/meuble/courte_duree) in metadata_json under 'apartment_subtype'.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold distinguishes maison_individuelle (single-family home) and maison_de_ville (townhouse). V2 has no subtype.
entries.qualification_impact: MEDIUM — two separate matrices for house subtypes
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'house'. Store subtype in metadata_json as individual or townhouse.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold distinguishes villa (large standalone) and villa_basse (single-story). V2 has no subtype.
entries.qualification_impact: MEDIUM — two matrices for villa subtypes
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'villa'. Store subtype (standard/basse) in metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold distinguishes studio (basic), studio_moderne (private shower), studio_meuble (furnished). V2 has no subtype.
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'studio'. Store subtype (basic/moderne/meuble) in metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold also has triplex (not in basic 11 types but in matrix catalog). V2 has no multi-level type concept.
entries.qualification_impact: LOW — one matrix for duplex, one for triplex
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'duplex'. Add 'triplex' as valid type.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold distinguishes chambre_simple (basic room), chambre_moderne (with shower), chambre_hotel (hotel room). V2 has no room-specific fields.
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'room'. Store subtype (simple/moderne/hotel) in metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold also has immeuble_de_rapport (income building) and immeuble_commercial (commercial building). V2 has no building-specific fields.
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'building'. Distinguish residential/commercial/mixed via metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 7 land subtypes based on title status and servicing. V2 has no land-specific fields (surface exists but is generic). Land-specific fields from Gold: is_agricultural, is_constructible, has_water, has_electricity, title_status.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — existing land entries must be classified into 7 subtypes
entries.search_impact: HIGH — cannot filter by title status
entries.security_impact: MEDIUM — land title status has legal implications
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'land'. Store subtype in metadata_json. Add title_status field. Add land-specific fields to metadata_json schema.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold boutique is a commercial type. V2 has no commercial-specific fields (front_window, parking_spots, has_signage).
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'shop'. Store commercial-specific fields in metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold lists entrepôt as industrial basic type but it also appears in commercial matrices. V2 has no industrial-specific fields.
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'warehouse'. Assign family based on context (industrial vs commercial).

entries.semantic_differences: Gold office is commercial type. V2 has no office-specific fields.
entries.recommended_resolution: Normalize to 'office'.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold's most basic residential type — single room, typically shared amenities. V2 has no concept of room vs apartment.
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'room' in property_type with metadata_json.subtype='basic'. Use metadata_json.shared_amenities boolean.

entries.semantic_differences: Room with private shower — bridges gap between basic room and studio
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'room' in property_type with metadata_json.subtype='moderne' and has_private_bathroom=true.

entries.semantic_differences: None — both treat studio as a basic self-contained unit
entries.recommended_resolution: Exact match — maintain 'studio' as valid property_type

entries.semantic_differences: Studio with internal shower vs basic studio with shared facilities — important distinction in Cameroonian market
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'studio' with metadata_json.subtype='moderne' and has_private_bathroom=true.

entries.semantic_differences: Furnished vs unfurnished — Gold has separate matrices; V2 has no is_furnished concept
entries.recommended_resolution: Add is_furnished boolean to Property model or metadata_json. Store subtype in metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold separates furnished/unfurnished at type level; V2 makes no distinction
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'apartment' with metadata_json.subtype='non_meuble' and is_furnished=false.

entries.semantic_differences: Furnished version of apartment
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'apartment' with metadata_json.subtype='meuble' and is_furnished=true.

entries.semantic_differences: Both treat villa as a standalone residential type
entries.recommended_resolution: Exact match — maintain 'villa' as valid property_type

entries.semantic_differences: Gold distinguishes single-story (basse) from multi-story villa; V2 does not
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'villa' with metadata_json.subtype='basse' or metadata_json.stories=1.

entries.semantic_differences: Both treat duplex as two-level unit
entries.recommended_resolution: Exact match — maintain 'duplex' as valid property_type

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has triplex as distinct type; not in basic 11 types but has dedicated matrix
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'triplex' as valid property_type or store as 'duplex' with metadata_json.levels=3.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold distinguishes individual house from townhouse; V2 does not
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'house' with metadata_json.subtype='individual'.

entries.semantic_differences: Townhouse — attached/row house in urban setting
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'house' with metadata_json.subtype='townhouse'.

entries.semantic_differences: Short-term hotel room rental — crosses residential and hotelier families
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'room' with metadata_json.subtype='hotel' and rental_type='short_term'.

entries.semantic_differences: Short-term furnished apartment — Airbnb-like rental
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'apartment' with metadata_json.rental_type='short_term' and is_furnished=true.

entries.semantic_differences: Furnished residence for both short and long-term rental — catch-all for serviced accommodation
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'apartment' with metadata_json.is_furnished=true and metadata_json.residence_type='serviced'.

entries.semantic_differences: Shared housing — multiple tenants per unit. V2 has no concept of shared tenancy.
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'room' or 'apartment' with metadata_json.occupancy='shared'. Add max_tenants field to metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Student housing — dedicated residence for university students. V2 has no educational housing concept.
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'residence' with metadata_json.subtype='university' or metadata_json.target_demographic='student'.

entries.semantic_differences: Land with valid title deed — highest-value land type. V2 has no title status concept.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — existing land entries need title status classification
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — cannot apply land title matrix
entries.security_impact: MEDIUM — title status is legally significant
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.title_status='titled'. Add title_status validation.

entries.semantic_differences: Land without formal title deed — lower value, higher risk
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.title_status='untitled'.

entries.semantic_differences: Land with basic services (water, electricity, roads)
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.serviced=true and metadata_json.title_status='loti'.

entries.semantic_differences: Raw land without services
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.serviced=false and metadata_json.title_status='non_loti'.

entries.semantic_differences: Land with collective/community title — common in rural areas
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.title_status='collective'.

entries.semantic_differences: Land with individual/private title — most marketable
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.title_status='individual'.

entries.semantic_differences: Land in process of being subdivided — transitional status
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.title_status='subdividing'.

entries.semantic_differences: Both treat boutique as commercial shop
entries.recommended_resolution: Maintain 'boutique' as valid property_type

entries.semantic_differences: Both treat bureau as office space
entries.recommended_resolution: Maintain 'bureau' as valid property_type

entries.semantic_differences: Generic commercial space — not a shop or office but general-purpose
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'commercial_space' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Retail store — larger than boutique
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'store' as valid property_type or store as 'shop' with metadata_json.subtype='store'.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold lists entrepot in both industrial basic types and commercial matrices
entries.recommended_resolution: Maintain as 'warehouse'. Family assignment determines industrial vs commercial context.

entries.semantic_differences: Large covered space — typically for storage or light industrial use
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'hangar' as valid property_type or store as 'warehouse' with metadata_json.subtype='hangar'.

entries.semantic_differences: Workshop space — for artisans, crafts, light manufacturing
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'workshop' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Restaurant/food service space
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'restaurant' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Bar/nightlife venue
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'bar' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has hotel as both commercial matrix type and dedicated hotelier family. V2 has no hotel type.
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'hotel' as valid property_type. Map to hotelier family when dedicated hotel operations exist.

entries.semantic_differences: Small hotel/guesthouse — differs from hotel in scale and service level
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'inn' as valid property_type or store as 'hotel' with metadata_json.subtype='auberge'.

entries.semantic_differences: Multi-unit building purchased for rental income — investment property
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'income_building' as valid property_type with investment metadata.

entries.semantic_differences: Multi-tenant commercial building — distinct from single commercial spaces
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'commercial_building' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Fuel/service station — specialized commercial property
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'gas_station' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Large industrial property — factory or industrial complex
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'industrial_site' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Venue for events, weddings, conferences
entries.recommended_resolution: Add 'event_space' as valid property_type.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 10 explicit steps including normalisation, classification, matching, mise en relation, suivi, conservation. V2 compresses to 5 statuses with no explicit workflow steps.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — existing statuses must map to workflow steps
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — workflow orchestration missing; steps 1-4, 7-8, 10 have no equivalents
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — qualification depends on workflow position
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — matching occurs at step 6 in Gold; V2 matches on published only
entries.CRM_impact: MEDIUM — CRM actions tied to workflow steps
entries.recommended_resolution: Extend status model to support workflow steps via metadata_json.workflow_step, or implement a WorkflowStep model.

entries.current_concept: 5 availability strings with no state machine
entries.semantic_differences: Gold has enforced transitions (available→pending→rented/sold→archived). V2 has 5 strings with no transition rules. Gold pending ≈ V2 reserved. Gold has no 'unavailable' state.
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — no transition enforcement; invalid transitions possible
entries.matching_impact: MEDIUM — exclusion rules filter archived/sold/rented but not enforced at state machine level
entries.recommended_resolution: Implement AVAILABILITY_TRANSITIONS similar to STATUS_TRANSITIONS. Add validation on availability changes.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has cron-based auto-archive rule; V2 has no inactivity tracking
entries.matching_impact: MEDIUM — stale properties remain in matching pool
entries.recommended_resolution: Implement cron job checking last_activity_at. Add last_activity_at field to Property if not present.

entries.current_concept: can_publish() checks: title, city, price, not deleted
entries.semantic_differences: Gold checks 8 rules (family, type coherence, location, price, détenteur, normalization, documents, code checks). V2 checks 3 rules (title, city, price). Missing: family, type coherence, détenteur, normalization, documents.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — existing properties may fail stricter rules
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — weaker validation means lower quality published listings
entries.recommended_resolution: Expand can_publish() to include family validation, type-family coherence, détenteur identification, and document presence checks.

entries.legacy_concept: 6-level pricing model (affiché, négociable, final, estimation, fourchette, historique)
entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 6 price levels: displayed, negotiable, final, estimation, market range, history. V2 only has min/max range.
entries.data_migration_impact: HIGH — price semantics lost in migration
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — cannot distinguish displayed vs final price, negotiable vs fixed
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — pricing matrices reference specific price types
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — budget matching uses only min/max; negotiation status unavailable
entries.recommended_resolution: Add price_type (listed/negotiable/final/estimated) enum. Store negotiable flag. Extend metadata_json for price history and estimation.

entries.legacy_concept: 7 additional price types (loyer, caution, avance, dépôt, mensualité, frais, taxes)
entries.semantic_differences: Gold has loyer (rent), caution (security deposit), avance (advance), dépôt de garantie (guarantee deposit), mensualité (monthly payment), frais de service (service fees), taxes. V2 has none of these.
entries.data_migration_impact: HIGH — all price types would need new fields
entries.search_impact: HIGH — cannot search by rent amount vs deposit
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — budget matching cannot distinguish rent vs purchase budget
entries.recommended_resolution: Add rent_price, deposit, service_fees fields to Property model. Store in metadata_json as interim.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has full scoring engine with 5 grades (A+ through D), 6 completeness fields, 5 source reliability levels. V2 has nothing.
entries.data_migration_impact: LOW — scoring can be calculated post-migration
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — no quality-based filtering or sorting
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — qualification depends on data quality
entries.search_impact: MEDIUM — cannot sort or filter by quality
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — matching would benefit from quality-weighted scoring
entries.recommended_resolution: Implement DataQualityEngine in Python. Add quality_score field to Property. Run scoring on create/update.

entries.legacy_concept: per-type specific fields (residential: rooms, floor, elevator, furnished; land: surface, constructible, utilities; commercial: window, parking, signage; industrial: loading, height, floor load)
entries.semantic_differences: Gold has 4 categories of type-specific fields with 20+ specialized attributes. V2 has 3 generic fields. Missing: floor, has_elevator, is_furnished, is_agricultural, is_constructible, has_water, has_electricity, front_window, parking_spots, has_signage, access_camion, hauteur_sous_plafond, charge_sol, quai_chargement, puissance_electrique.
entries.data_migration_impact: HIGH — type-specific data must be extracted and stored
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — business rules requiring specific fields cannot execute
entries.search_impact: HIGH — cannot filter by type-specific criteria
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — matching limited to generic dimensions
entries.recommended_resolution: Store all type-specific fields in metadata_json with validated schema. Add most-used fields (is_furnished, floor, has_elevator) as first-class columns.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold has opération_autorisée as common attribute. V2 has no transaction type on Property. Transaction intent exists only on Project model.
entries.data_migration_impact: MEDIUM — transaction type must be inferred from context
entries.runtime_impact: HIGH — cannot filter properties by transaction type at schema level
entries.search_impact: HIGH — cannot query 'for rent' vs 'for sale'
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — budget tolerance differs by transaction type (±20% rent, ±15% buy, ±25% invest)
entries.CRM_impact: HIGH — lead scoring differentiates by transaction type
entries.recommended_resolution: Add transaction_type enum to Property schema: rent, buy, sell, invest. This is critical for matching correctness.

entries.legacy_concept: 5 minimum fields (type, transaction, price, city, description)
entries.current_concept: can_publish() checks title, city, price; create validates summary
entries.semantic_differences: Gold requires type, transaction, price, city, description. V2 requires title, summary, city, price (for publish). Missing: explicit type requirement, transaction requirement.
entries.runtime_impact: MEDIUM — property_type not strictly required in can_publish()
entries.recommended_resolution: Add property_type and transaction_type checks to can_publish().

entries.current_concept: property_type matching possible via free string
entries.semantic_differences: Gold matching engine gives property_type 15% weight and has per-type specific weights (standard/reduced/minimal). V2 has no type-based matching logic.
entries.matching_impact: HIGH — type dimension missing from matching
entries.CRM_impact: MEDIUM — property_type_match is 20% of CRM scoring
entries.recommended_resolution: Implement property type matching using normalized type values. The matching engine must compare normalized types, not raw strings.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold détenteur is the holder of the record (could be agent, owner, etc.). V2 ownerOrganizationId links to an Organization but is nullable and organization-only.
entries.recommended_resolution: Add holder_type (agent/owner/agency) and holder_id to Property metadata_json.

entries.semantic_differences: Gold investment type for rental property investment. V2 has no investment concept on Property model. Project model has investment-like fields but is not linked to Property.
entries.qualification_impact: HIGH — investment matrices cannot be applied
entries.matching_impact: MEDIUM — investor intent exists in CRM but no property-side investment tagging
entries.CRM_impact: HIGH — diaspora_investor and investor lead types map to investment properties
entries.recommended_resolution: Add investment_type field to metadata_json or create Property-Project link for investment properties.

entries.semantic_differences: Land purchased specifically for investment/appreciation
entries.recommended_resolution: Store as 'land' with metadata_json.investment_type='land_investment'.

entries.semantic_differences: Commercial property purchased for return on investment
entries.recommended_resolution: Add investment metadata to commercial property types.

entries.semantic_differences: Investment in property development/construction project
entries.recommended_resolution: Link to Project model with project_type='development'.

entries.semantic_differences: Condominium/strata management entity — not a property listing but an investment/management structure
entries.recommended_resolution: Out of property scope — maps to Organization or a new Condominium model.

critical_gaps: No property families — 7 Gold families have no V2 equivalent
No type enum — 52+ property types are free-form strings
No transaction type — matching cannot distinguish rent/buy budgets
high_priority_gaps: Per-type specific fields missing — 20+ specialized attributes not represented
medium_priority_gaps: No availability state machine — transitions unenforced
No auto-archive rule — stale properties never cleaned
No data quality scoring — quality-based ranking impossible

recommended_immediate_actions.rationale: Enables family validation, qualification matrix selection, family-based business rules
Critical for matching engine budget tolerance (rent ±20%, buy ±15%, invest ±25%)
Enables type validation on create; foundation for all type-based logic
Prevents invalid availability changes; enables workflow automation

recommended_immediate_actions.action: Expand can_publish() to include family, type, and transaction checks
recommended_immediate_actions.rationale: Improves listing quality; aligns with Gold publication rules

recommended_immediate_actions.action: Create metadata_json schema for storing all Gold-specific fields
recommended_immediate_actions.rationale: Preserves granularity without schema changes; enables progressive migration
Enables quality-based ranking and matching improvement
